results.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a brand.

DragonForce launched on BreachForums on December 6, 2023 — and within 18 months had claimed 363 victims across 30+ countries.

This is a cartel. Complete with a logo, affiliate contracts, an 80/20 revenue split, and a recruitment pipeline that absorbed RansomHub's entire operation on April 8, 2025.

They didn't hide in the dark. They marketed themselves.

The circuit dragon isn't a mascot. It's a warning.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythological dragon rendered in bleeding-edge circuit-board aesthetics makes DragonForce instantly recognizable as a professional criminal enterprise — not a script kiddie operation.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
This is what 363 victims read after their systems went dark.

DragonForce's ransom note isn't random. It's engineered.

ChaCha8 encryption. BYOVD to kill your EDR using truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys. Cross-platform reach — Windows, Linux, ESXi — nothing left standing.

The note arrives after your defenses are already dead.

M&S, Co-op, Harrods. Three British institutions. One summer. £300M+ in combined impact.

This is what industrialized ransomware looks like at scale.

#DragonForce #RansomwareAnalysis #BYOVD #CyberThreat #IncidentResponse
results.hook: Showing the actual anatomy of a DragonForce ransom note — with real technical labels — turns abstract cybercrime into visceral, documented reality that security professionals must share.

results.caption:
Nobody can confirm where DragonForce actually comes from.

Early posts showed hacktivist language. Malaysian threat actor chatter. Then pure RaaS criminality with professional affiliate contracts.

Nation-state cover? Ideological smokescreen? Or just money?

What we know: they debuted on BreachForums December 6, 2023. By March 19, 2025 they had declared themselves a cartel. By April 8, 2025 they had absorbed RansomHub's entire operation.

363 victims. 30+ countries. And an origin story nobody can verify.

The most dangerous threat actors are the ones who control their own narrative.

#DragonForce #ThreatIntelligence #Attribution #Ransomware #CyberEspionage
results.hook: The haunting ambiguity of DragonForce's true origins — nation-state, hacktivist, or pure criminal enterprise — creates the kind of unresolved tension that drives explosive LinkedIn debate.
